App.Title: WinBeacon
App.Description: Understand Windows errors in plain language
Lang.Label: Language
Theme.Label: Theme
Theme.Hint: The theme applies immediately to the whole interface.
Lang.RestartHint: Language applies immediately. Refresh the list if needed.
Timeline.Title: Activity over the last 24 h
Options.AdvancedHint: Shows telemetry, cloud AI, and technical incident details.
Empty.NoSeverity.Title: No incidents of this type
Empty.NoSeverity.Message: Try another severity filter or choose All.
Snapshot.Title: Compare over time
Options.Title: Options
Options.AiKeyHint: API key: EVENTVIEWER_AI_API_KEY environment variable (recommended). Otherwise stored encrypted (DPAPI).
Maintenance.Title: Maintenance
Maintenance.Hint: Useful actions for a specific issue. Admin + confirmation required.
Admin.Title: Administrator rights recommended
Admin.Message: To read all Windows logs and run maintenance, launch the app as administrator.
Detail.SelectMeaning: Choose a row on the left to read a simple explanation.
Tag.Hardware.Advice: Possible hardware issue. Check disk health, back up important data, then run a disk check if needed.
Tag.Network.Advice: Possible network issue. Check your connection, restart the router if needed, then try another DNS server.
Tag.Memory.Advice: Possible memory issue. Close unused apps and restart the computer if needed.
Tag.Service.Advice: A Windows service had a problem. A restart may help; otherwise check the event details.
Tag.Security.Advice: Security-related alert. Run a Windows Defender scan and check recent sign-ins if needed.
Empty.NoIncidents.Title: No recent incidents
Empty.NoIncidents.Message: Good news: no errors or warnings in this log.
Empty.NoResults.Title: No results
Empty.NoResults.Message: Try another keyword (e.g. disk, network, memory).
Empty.NoNew.Title: No new incidents
Empty.NoNew.Message: Nothing new since your last comparison point.
About.Title: About
About.Creator: Created by Marco Barbaro
Confirm.Maintenance.FlushDns.Title: Flush DNS cache
Confirm.Maintenance.FlushDns.Message: Quick and safe action to flush the Windows DNS cache. Continue?
Confirm.Maintenance.Sfc.Title: Repair system files (SFC)
Confirm.Maintenance.Sfc.Message: Runs sfc /scannow. This can take several minutes and requires admin rights. Continue?
Confirm.Maintenance.ResetNetwork.Title: Reset network
Confirm.Maintenance.ResetNetwork.Message: Resets the Windows network stack (Winsock + TCP/IP). Internet may drop briefly and a reboot may be required. Continue?
About.DesktopEdition: USB / desktop edition (maintenance available if admin).
Action.InProgressLong: {0} in progress… This may take several minutes.
Action.SfcConsoleHint: A Command Prompt window opens to show SFC progress. Do not close it.
Status.CloudConsentOn: Cloud consent: incident details may be sent to the configured HTTPS endpoint.
Status.CloudConsentOff: Cloud consent denied — the assistant stays local.
Status.LimitedWithoutAdmin: Some data may be limited without administrator rights
Empty.LoadFailed.Title: Unable to read logs
Empty.LoadFailed.AdminRequired: Run the app as administrator to read Windows logs.
Export.NoData: No incidents to export. Refresh first.
Snapshot.NoneToCompare: No comparison point. Save one first.
Feedback.NotUseful: Thanks — we'll note this wasn't useful.
Cloud.Status.ConsentRequired: AI cloud: consent required before sending
Cloud.Status.EndpointRejected: AI cloud: endpoint rejected (public HTTPS only)
TechDetail.Dcom.Title: Technical detail (DCOM 10016)
TechDetail.Dcom.MissingDll: Windows could not display the official event text (missing message DLL).
TechDetail.Dcom.ExtractedIntro: Useful information extracted from the event:
TechDetail.Dcom.BenignNote: Note: these DCOM alerts are very often benign (Microsoft components).
TechDetail.Generic.Title: Technical detail
TechDetail.Generic.MissingDll: The official Windows text is unavailable (missing message DLL).
AutoFix.Execute.None: No automatic action for this incident.
AutoFix.None.SelectExplanation: Choose a row to suggest a fix.
AutoFix.Dcom.Title: Usually no action needed
AutoFix.Dcom.Explanation: DCOM / COM permission alerts are often benign. No automatic network fix.
AutoFix.Dns.Title: Flush DNS cache
AutoFix.Dns.Explanation: Often enough when browsing or name resolution fails.
AutoFix.Dns.Confirm: We'll flush the Windows DNS cache. Quick and safe.
AutoFix.NetworkSettings.Title: Check the network
AutoFix.NetworkSettings.ExplanationStore: Opens Windows network settings. For automatic DNS flush, use the USB edition.
AutoFix.ResetNetwork.Title: Reset network stack
AutoFix.ResetNetwork.Explanation: Resets Windows networking (Winsock + TCP/IP). A restart may be required afterwards.
AutoFix.ResetNetwork.Confirm: Reset the network now? Internet may drop briefly. A restart may be required.
AutoFix.NetworkSettings.ExplanationStore: Opens network settings. Full reset is available in the USB edition.
AutoFix.NetworkDns.Title: First network step
AutoFix.NetworkDns.Explanation: We start by flushing the DNS cache — the safest fix.
AutoFix.NetworkSettings.ExplanationFirst: Opens network settings to check connection / Wi‑Fi / resolution.
AutoFix.DiskCleanup.Title: Disk Cleanup
AutoFix.DiskCleanup.Explanation: Opens the Windows cleanup tool to reclaim space.
AutoFix.Storage.Title: Check disk space
AutoFix.Storage.Explanation: Opens Windows Storage settings to see what uses space.
AutoFix.Defender.Title: Windows Defender quick scan
AutoFix.Defender.Explanation: Runs a quick scan to check for remaining threats.
AutoFix.Defender.Confirm: Start a Windows Defender quick scan?
AutoFix.Update.ExplanationStoreSecurity: In the Store edition, open Windows Update / Windows Security manually.
AutoFix.Update.Title: Check for updates
AutoFix.Update.Explanation: Opens Windows Update to retry installation.
AutoFix.Bugcheck.Title: Understand what crashed
AutoFix.Bugcheck.Explanation: Opens Windows Reliability History to see programs and drivers linked to the blue screen. Next: updates, OEM drivers, SFC.
AutoFix.Drivers.Title: Check drivers
AutoFix.Drivers.Explanation: Opens Device Manager to spot faulty hardware, then update the driver.
AutoFix.Sfc.Title: System file check
AutoFix.Sfc.Explanation: SFC finds and repairs corrupted Windows files. This can take 10 to 30 minutes.
AutoFix.Sfc.Confirm: Run SFC /scannow? Leave the PC on — this can take a while.
AutoFix.Update.ExplanationStoreSfc: SFC repair is not available in the Store. Update Windows, then restart.
AutoFix.Memory.Title: Close apps then restart
AutoFix.Memory.Explanation: For memory issues, the safest step is to close heavy apps then restart the PC.
AutoFix.Update.ExplanationFallback: For this incident, open Windows Update then restart if the issue continues.
AutoFix.Manual.Title: Manual fix
AutoFix.Manual.Explanation: For this incident, follow the “What to do” advice. No safe automatic action is available.
Ai.BetaLocal: AI beta mode (local explanation — cloud not used for this reply)
Event.Unknown.Title: Windows event {0}
Event.Unknown.Description: System event not listed in the knowledge base. Source: {0}
Event.Unknown.Solution: Look up this event ID in Microsoft documentation or use the AI summary for more details.
Event.Wer.Title: Windows Error Report (WER)
Event.Wer.Description: An error report was generated after an application crash.
Event.Wer.Solution:
1) Note the application name in the technical details.
2) Update or reinstall the application.
3) Check Windows Update and graphics drivers.
4) If it repeats, uninstall recent add-ons.
Event.41.Title: Unexpected system restart
Event.41.Description: The system restarted without shutting down cleanly. This can be caused by a power cut, a system crash, or a forced update.
Event.41.Solution: Check the power supply, hardware drivers, and recent Windows updates.
Event.1074.Title: Planned system shutdown or restart
Event.1074.Description: The system shut down or restarted in a planned way (by a user, an app, or Windows Update).
Event.1074.Solution: No action needed if this was intentional.
Event.6008.Title: Unexpected system shutdown
Event.6008.Description: The system shut down unexpectedly during the last boot.
Event.6008.Solution: Check the logs to identify the cause (hardware, overheating, power).
Event.10016.Title: Insufficient DCOM permissions
Event.10016.Description: An application is trying to access a DCOM component without the required permissions. The component name and GUID are shown when Windows provides them.
Event.10016.Solution: Usually harmless (often a Microsoft service such as Windows Update Medic). No action needed in most cases.
Event.7.Title: Device error
Event.7.Description: A storage device reported a hardware error. This may indicate a failing drive.
Event.7.Solution: Back up your data immediately and check the drive with CrystalDiskInfo or chkdsk.
Event.11.Title: Disk controller error
Event.11.Description: The controller detected an error on the hard drive.
Event.11.Solution: Test the drive with the manufacturer tools. Replace it if needed.
Event.51.Title: Paging error
Event.51.Description: An error occurred while writing a memory page to disk.
Event.51.Solution: Check free disk space and the integrity of the page file.
Event.153.Title: Low disk space
Event.153.Description: A disk volume is running low on free space.
Event.153.Solution: Free up space by deleting unused files or using Disk Cleanup.
Event.4201.Title: Network connection lost
Event.4201.Description: The network connection was interrupted unexpectedly.
Event.4201.Solution: Check network cables, the router, and network adapter settings.
Event.8003.Title: DNS resolution problem
Event.8003.Description: The DNS resolver cannot contact DNS servers.
Event.8003.Solution: Check DNS settings (8.8.8.8 for Google DNS) and Internet connectivity.
Event.4625.Title: Sign-in failure
Event.4625.Description: A sign-in attempt failed (wrong password or locked account).
Event.4625.Solution: Check the credentials. If repeated, it may indicate an intrusion attempt.
Event.4624.Title: Successful sign-in
Event.4648.Title: Sign-in with explicit credentials
Event.4648.Description: A user tried to sign in using different credentials.
Event.4648.Solution: Normal if you use Run as administrator or network connections.
Event.19.Title: Update install failure
Event.19.Description: Windows Update could not install an update.
Event.19.Solution: Run the Windows Update troubleshooter or try again later.
Event.20.Title: Update download failure
Event.20.Description: Windows Update could not download updates.
Event.20.Solution: Check your Internet connection and free disk space.
Event.1000.Title: Application crash
Event.1000.Solution: Reinstall the app, update drivers, or check for corrupted files.
Event.1002.Title: Application hang
Event.1002.Solution: Check whether the app is up to date and compatible with your Windows version.
Event.219.Title: Driver start failure
Event.219.Description: A device driver could not load at startup.
Event.219.Solution:
1) Open Device Manager and look for an exclamation mark.
2) Update the driver (or motherboard/GPU drivers from the manufacturer site).
3) Check Windows Update for optional drivers.
4) If it started after an install, uninstall the recent driver.
Event.1001.Title: Bugcheck verification error
Event.1001.Description: The system detected a critical error (blue screen / BSOD) and restarted to protect itself.
Event.1001.Solution:
1) Install Windows updates (fixes and drivers).
2) Open Reliability History to see which program or driver failed just before.
3) Update GPU, chipset, and storage drivers (PC/motherboard maker site).
4) Run a system file repair (SFC).
5) Test memory (Windows Memory Diagnostic) if crashes continue.
6) As a last resort, analyze the dump (BlueScreenView / WinDbg) for the exact driver.
Event.2004.Title: System resource problem
Event.2004.Description: The system is low on resources (memory or handles).
Event.2004.Solution: Close unused apps or add more RAM.
Event.7000.Title: Service start failure
Event.7000.Description: A Windows service could not start.
Event.7000.Solution: Check service dependencies and permissions in services.msc.
Event.7001.Title: Dependent service not started
Event.7001.Description: A service cannot start because a service it depends on is not running.
Event.7001.Solution: Identify and start the dependent service first.
Event.7009.Title: Service timeout exceeded
Event.7009.Description: A service took too long to respond to a start or control request.
Event.7009.Solution: The service may be stuck. Restart the system or disable the problematic service.
Event.7011.Title: Service transactional timeout
Event.7011.Description: A service did not respond within the allotted time during a transaction.
Event.7011.Solution: Check system performance and the specific service logs.
Event.7031.Title: Service terminated unexpectedly
Event.7031.Solution: Configure the service recovery options in services.msc.
Event.7034.Title: Service terminated unexpectedly (repeated)
Event.7034.Description: A service stopped for no apparent reason.
Event.7034.Solution: Check the application logs for more details about the cause.
Event.2019.Title: Insufficient space for the page file
Event.2019.Description: The system lacks space to grow the page file.
Event.2019.Solution: Free disk space or increase the page file size.
Event.10110.Title: USB power problem
Event.10110.Description: A USB device is requesting more power than the port can supply.
Event.10110.Solution: Use a powered USB hub or connect the device directly to the PC.
Event.10.Title: Expired or invalid SSL/TLS certificate
Event.10.Description: A security certificate used for an HTTPS connection is invalid.
Event.10.Solution: Update root certificates or contact the service administrator.
Event.1.Title: System time change
Event.1.Solution: Check that NTP time sync is working correctly.
Event.1116.Title: Threat detected by Windows Defender
Event.1116.Description: Windows Defender detected malware or potentially unwanted software.
Event.1116.Solution: Follow Windows Defender recommendations to remove or quarantine the threat.
Event.1117.Title: Action taken on a threat
Event.1117.Description: Windows Defender took action against a detected threat.
Event.1117.Solution: Verify the threat was handled correctly in the protection history.
Event.24.Title: BitLocker volume locked
Event.24.Description: A BitLocker-encrypted volume is locked and needs a recovery key.
Event.24.Solution: Use your BitLocker recovery key to unlock the volume.
Event.372.Title: Print spooler error
Event.372.Description: The print spooler service encountered a problem.
Event.372.Solution: Restart the Print Spooler service or reinstall printer drivers.
